response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems developer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role's hard requirements. There is no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency (a strict requirement), only brief C/C++ experience (~6 months at National Instruments versus years required for UNIX C development), and no demonstrated Linux kernel analysis or modification experience. The background centers on web platforms, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang rather than embedded networking, L2/L3 switch development, or security technologies like MACsec and 802.1X.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-performance services in DLang) and Linux familiarity
concerns: No evidence of Japanese language proficiency, which is a non-negotiable requirement
Minimal professional C experience and no Linux kernel investigation/modification background
No exposure to switch/embedded networking or security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP)

key_factors.factor: Lack of C/UNIX and Linux kernel development depth
General seniority and systems programming aptitude
